Flex 5000® Adapter - Denial of Service

Vulnerability Exploitability Exchange
format:
JSON
Summary
The security of our products is important to us as your industrial automation supplier. This security issue was found internally during routine testing and is being reported based on our commitment to customer transparency and improvement of all business environments.
Product Description
The FLEX 5000® EtherNet/IP Adapters from Rockwell Automation are high-performance distributed I/O adapters that connect FLEX 5000® I/O modules to EtherNet/IP networks, supporting advanced network topologies including Device Level Ring (DLR) for industrial automation applications.
Affected Products and Solution
Affected Product
CVE
Affected Software Version
Corrected in Software Version
Affected Catalog Numbers
FLEX 5000® Adapter
CVE-2026-12659
v6.011
v6.012

Security Issue Details for CVE-2026-12659
Category
Details
CVE ID
CVE-2026-12659
Impact
A denial-of-service security issue exists in the affected products. The security issue stems from improper handling of exceptional conditions when processing crafted CIP packets sent to the adapter. A power cycle is required to recover the module and associated I/O.
CVSS 3.1 Base Score
7.5/10
CVSS 4.0 Base Score
8.7/10
CWE
CWE-415: Double Free
Known Exploited Vulnerability
No
Mitigations and Workarounds
Customers using the affected software, who are not able to upgrade to one of the corrected versions, should use our
security best practices
.

Glossary:
·
Denial-of-Service (DoS):
An attack that disrupts the normal functioning of a system, often by overwhelming it with requests.
·
CIP Communication:
Common Industrial Protocol (CIP) is a common communication standard that is widely used in industrial automation. Comprises a series of protocols for communication between different devices and systems in automation technology
·
Module:
A self-contained unit within a system that performs a specific function and can operate independently or as part of a larger system
